val.description: Declares an immutable value binding in ATS/Postiats, binding a name to the result of an expression with optional type annotation. Values are evaluated eagerly and cannot be reassigned after initialization.

fun.description: Declares a recursive function in ATS/Postiats with explicit parameter types and return type. Unlike fn, fun allows self-referential calls enabling direct recursion within the function body.

fn.description: Declares a non-recursive function in ATS/Postiats. The compiler verifies that fn functions do not call themselves, providing a guarantee of termination for simple non-recursive procedures.

var.description: Declares a mutable variable in ATS/Postiats that can be updated with assignment operations. Variables require linear type tracking and proof obligations to ensure safe memory access.

datatype.signature: datatype name = Constructor1 of type | Constructor2 of type
datatype.description: Declares an algebraic data type in ATS/Postiats with named constructors for pattern matching. Datatypes are garbage-collected and support polymorphism through type parameters.

datavtype.signature: datavtype name = Constructor1 of type | Constructor2 of type
datavtype.description: Declares a linear algebraic data type (viewtype) in ATS/Postiats that must be explicitly freed. Linear datatypes enable safe manual memory management enforced by the type system.

sortdef.description: Defines a named sort abbreviation in the statics of ATS/Postiats. Sorts classify types and are used in dependent type specifications to constrain static indices for verified programming.

implement.description: Provides the implementation for a previously declared function template or interface function in ATS/Postiats, separating specification from implementation for modular program design.

stadef.description: Defines a static abbreviation in ATS/Postiats for use in dependent types and sort expressions. Static definitions enable reusable type-level computations and index expressions.

typedef.description: Creates a type alias in ATS/Postiats, providing a shorter name for a complex type expression. Type definitions improve readability and enable reuse of common type patterns.

prval.description: Declares a proof value in ATS/Postiats that exists only at compile time for theorem proving. Proof values are erased during compilation and have zero runtime cost.

prfun.description: Declares a proof function in ATS/Postiats that transforms proofs at compile time. Proof functions witness logical implications and are entirely erased from the generated executable code.

let_in_end.description: A scoping construct in ATS/Postiats that introduces local declarations visible only within the expression between in and end. Enables structured local bindings with controlled visibility.

case.signature: case expression of pattern1 => result1 | pattern2 => result2
case.description: Pattern matching expression in ATS/Postiats that deconstructs values against patterns and executes the first matching branch. The compiler checks exhaustiveness to prevent unhandled cases.

String.description: Text data type representing a sequence of characters.

Number.description: Numeric data type for integer and floating-point values.

Boolean.description: Logical type with two values: true and false.

Array.description: Ordered collection of elements accessible by index.

Object.description: Collection of key-value pairs (properties and methods).

null.description: Represents the intentional absence of any value.

undefined.description: Represents an uninitialized or missing value.

print.description: Output a value to the standard output stream.
